channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenyan Military Doctor Gladys Kipsang Celebrates 13 Years of Dedicated Service in the US Army
item.description: Kenyan-born orthopaedic surgeon and US Army officer, Captain Gladys Kipsang, has celebrated an impressive milestone of 13 years serving in the United States military. Kipsang, who hails from Kenya and...

item.title: Asu Beks Urges Presidency to Hand Over NIMASA’s Idle Floating Dockyard to Private Operator
item.description: Elder Asu Beks, wey be CEO of Maritime Media Limited and convener of the Maritime Industry Merit Awards, don call President Bola Ahmed Tinubu make government concession the abandoned floating dockyard...

item.title: SoftBank Announces $710 Billion Ohio Data Centre Campus
item.description: Japanese investment giant SoftBank Group is embarking on a monumental infrastructure project in Ohio, representing a staggering $US500 billion investment, which translates to approximately $710 billio...

item.title: MWO advises OFWs to be cautious of fraudulent repatriation offers
item.description: The Migrant Workers Office (MWO) in Dubai and the Northern Emirates has issued a warning to overseas Filipino workers (OFWs) regarding scams related to repatriation assistance. Fraudsters have reporte...

item.title: Nigerian Army Detains Brig. Gen. Archibong and Staff Over Links to Retired General’s Terror Finance Investigation
item.description: The Nigerian Army don hold Brigadier General Gabriel E. Archibong together with him domestic staff – him cook/gardener, personal assistant, and driver – because of connections with retired Major Gener...

item.title: Gautam Singhania Rescued After Speedboat Accident in Maldives, Recovering in Mumbai
item.description: Gautam Hari Singhania, the Chairman and Managing Director of Raymond Group, was involved in a speedboat accident in the Maldives on Thursday night. The boat, carrying at least seven people including t...

item.title: Hamilton Selected as New Location for New York Islanders’ AHL Team
item.description: The city of Hamilton, Ontario, has been announced as the new home for the American Hockey League (AHL) affiliate of the New York Islanders. The relocation represents a strategic move by the Islanders ...

item.title: US Increases Military Presence in Middle East Amid Iran Tensions
item.description: The United States is sending thousands of extra troops to the Middle East, according to three US officials, as tensions with Iran continue to escalate. These reinforcements include the USS Boxer, an a...

item.title: ICE Arrests Defendant During Federal Court Hearing, Raising Legal Concerns
item.description: In an unusual and controversial incident in Los Angeles, Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents removed a criminal defendant, Orlando Olivar, from a federal courtroom immediately after a pre...

item.title: Trump signals U.S. support for Israel amid ongoing conflict
item.description: In a brief but telling exchange with reporters, U.S. President Donald Trump indicated that he does not support an immediate ceasefire in the ongoing conflict involving Israel. When asked whether Israe...

item.title: President Ruto Defends Administration Against Opposition Criticism
item.description: President William Ruto has hit back at the opposition, saying he is just getting started with his development agenda for Kenya. Speaking recently, Ruto dismissed claims that his government is failing ...

item.title: EFF supports ANC budgets in Johannesburg council after earlier opposition threats
item.description: In a notable turn of events, the Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) decided to back the African National Congress (ANC) in passing key budgets within the City of Johannesburg council on Friday, despite p...

item.title: Highlights and Public Response to Pierre Poilievre’s Interview on the Joe Rogan Podcast
item.description: On March 20, 2026, Pierre Poilievre, leader of Canada's Conservative Party, appeared on the Joe Rogan Experience podcast, discussing a wide array of topics, from Canadian politics and leadership to li...

item.title: Mkuyuni Bridge Nears Completion, Govt Confirms Safe Use
item.description: The Tanzanian government has expressed satisfaction with the ongoing construction of bridges across the country, especially the Mkuyuni Bridge in Mwanza. The Minister for Works, Abdallah Ulega, visite...

item.title: Canadian Firefighter from Winnipeg Wins World’s Strongest Firefighter Title for the First Time
item.description: Evan Yaworski, a firefighter with the Royal Canadian Air Force based in Winnipeg and originally from Saskatoon, has made history by becoming the first Canadian to win the World’s Strongest Firefighter...

item.title: Matatu carrying Kenyatta University students overturns, killing three
item.description: A matatu that was ferrying Kenyatta University (KU) students overturned in a road accident, leaving three people dead and several others injured. The incident happened along a busy highway as the vehi...

item.title: Cape Town's Budget Allocation for Housing, Roads, and the Proposed N2 Wall
item.description: The City of Cape Town's budget plans for the next three years include significant investments in infrastructure, with housing, roadworks, and the controversial N2 Edge wall project among the key initi...

item.title: Coroner finds rural doctor delayed critical transfer leading to Indigenous woman's death
item.description: The family of Eve Brown, a 42-year-old Warrimay woman, has highlighted the ongoing health inequities faced by Indigenous Australians after her death at Lightning Ridge Multi-Purpose Centre in northwes...

item.title: Election Commission sets up 19 appellate tribunals for SIR-related voter appeals in Bengal
item.description: In New Delhi, the Election Commission, following directions from the Supreme Court and recommendations by the Chief Justice of Calcutta High Court, has established 19 appellate tribunals in West Benga...

item.title: Farage attempts to distance himself from Trump amid public disapproval
item.description: Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, is attempting to publicly distance himself from former US President Donald Trump, a man he has long admired and associated with politically. For over a decade, Farag...
